description: Postman is my API testing command center — I build collections, chain requests, manage environments, and run automated monitors that validate every endpoint I ship.
I write detailed Postman test scripts with status code checks, schema validations, response time assertions, and dynamic variable extractions baked into every request.
Rest Assured is my Java API testing powerhouse — its fluent BDD-style syntax makes writing readable, maintainable HTTP assertions a genuinely pleasant experience.
TestNG orchestrates my Java API test suites with parallel execution, grouped test runs, and data providers that drive hundreds of parameterized scenarios from a single test.
JUnit underpins my unit and integration test layers — lifecycle hooks, parameterized tests, and extension points give me precise control over every test execution.
BDD is how I align testing with business intent — writing Given-When-Then scenarios that non-technical stakeholders can read, review, and sign off on directly.
Cucumber turns acceptance criteria into living documentation — I wire Gherkin feature files to step definitions that execute real API calls and assert real responses.
I build Jenkins pipelines that trigger API test suites on every merge — with stage-gated deployments that only proceed when all contract and regression tests pass.
I design data-driven test frameworks using Excel, JSON, and CSV bindings — one test method covering hundreds of input combinations without duplicating a single line of code.
I use SoapUI for SOAP and REST service testing in enterprise environments — load tests, security scans, and mock services are all part of my SoapUI toolkit.
JMeter is my load testing workhorse — I simulate thousands of concurrent API users, analyze throughput trends, and identify bottlenecks before traffic spikes hit production.
Karate DSL lets me write API tests without a single line of Java — its unified syntax for HTTP, assertions, and mocking makes it incredibly fast to adopt and scale.
I generate polished Extent HTML reports after every test run — with test status summaries, failure screenshots, environment metadata, and historical trend comparisons.
Allure transforms raw test results into beautiful stakeholder-ready reports — step-level breakdowns, request-response logs, and build-over-build trend analytics all in one view.
When off-the-shelf reports don't cut it, I build custom HTML dashboards from scratch — tailored metrics, branded layouts, and interactive charts for client-facing test summaries.
JSON is the universal language of APIs I speak fluently — from crafting complex nested request bodies to parsing deeply nested responses in automated assertions.
Newman brings Postman collections to the command line — I integrate it into CI pipelines so API regression suites run headlessly on every push with full HTML reports.
I script k6 performance tests in JavaScript to stress-test APIs under realistic load profiles — surfacing p95 latency spikes and error rates before they reach production.
WireMock lets me stub third-party APIs and test edge cases that real services can't reliably reproduce — fault injection, delays, and custom error scenarios all on demand.
Pact keeps my microservices honest — consumer-driven contract tests ensure that API providers never silently break the consumers depending on them across deployments.
I treat OpenAPI specs as the single source of truth for API contracts — generating interactive docs, client SDKs, and test stubs directly from the specification.
I validate API response structures against JSON schemas automatically — catching missing fields, wrong types, and unexpected nulls before they silently break downstream consumers.
I test OAuth 2.0 flows end-to-end — authorization code, client credentials, and token refresh scenarios — ensuring secure API access works flawlessly across all grant types.
I decode, validate, and manipulate JWTs in test scenarios — verifying claims, testing token expiry edge cases, and ensuring auth headers behave correctly across secured endpoints.
Apache HttpClient gives me low-level HTTP control in Java — custom headers, connection pooling, SSL configuration, and retry logic for precise API interaction in test frameworks.
Retrofit turns REST API calls into clean Java interfaces — I use it for type-safe HTTP clients in Android and backend test harnesses with minimal boilerplate.
Axios is my JavaScript HTTP client of choice — interceptors, automatic JSON parsing, and clean promise-based syntax make it indispensable in both frontend and Node.js test scripts.
OkHttp is my efficient Java HTTP client for test automation — connection pooling, transparent GZIP, and response caching keep my API test suites fast and resource-friendly.
I containerize entire API test environments with Docker — isolated, reproducible test runs that behave identically on a developer laptop and a CI server at 2am.
Every API test suite I own runs automatically through GitHub Actions — triggered on PRs, scheduled nightly, and gating deployments when critical contract tests fail.
I configure GitLab CI pipelines with dedicated API test stages — parallel job execution, artifact publishing, and environment-specific test configs that scale across teams.
I schedule Postman Monitors to run collection tests on a cadence against live environments — catching API regressions and SLA breaches around the clock without manual intervention.
MockServer lets me simulate any HTTP dependency in isolation — I define expectations, verify interactions, and test failure scenarios that would be impossible with real services.
Gatling is my Scala-powered performance testing tool — I write simulation scripts that model real user traffic patterns and generate detailed HTML performance reports automatically.
I stream performance test metrics into InfluxDB for time-series analysis — pairing it with Grafana dashboards to visualize latency trends and throughput patterns across test runs.
